title: Plant-Milk Rice Kheer
description: Creamy festive rice pudding with soy or oat milk, cardamom, and nuts. Dairy-free celebration dessert.
explanation: Kheer is a festival and Sunday staple. Soy milk gives more protein and body; oat milk is naturally sweeter; a splash of coconut milk at the end adds richness without dairy.
Slow simmering reduces the milk and softens rice into the classic texture—do not rush on high heat.
ingredients: 1 liter Soy Milk or Oat Milk (unsweetened)
instructions: Wash rice and soak 15 minutes. Drain.
Bring plant milk to a gentle simmer. Add rice and cardamom.
Cook on low, stirring often, 30–40 minutes until rice is soft and kheer thickens.
Add jaggery after turning heat very low (or off briefly) so milk does not split.
Stir in nuts, raisins, saffron, and coconut milk. Serve warm or chilled.
stepNotes: Jaggery can curdle some plant milks if boiled hard—melt it in at the end.
Stir frequently so rice does not stick to the bottom.
Kheer thickens more as it cools; keep it slightly looser than your final target.
Saffron soaked in 1 tbsp warm milk gives better color.
Chilled kheer is excellent the next day for festival prep.

variationSections.title: Grain swaps
items: Sabudana kheer with soaked tapioca pearls.
Vermicelli (seviyan) for a faster festival sweet.
Quinoa or millets for a modern twist.

variationSections.title: Sweetener and richness
items: Dates blended into the milk instead of sugar.
More coconut milk for a richer Diwali bowl.
Less sweetener and more cardamom if serving after a heavy meal.
